doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:

Right middle lobe:
-Peribronchovascular micronodules measuring up to 3 mm on images 177-194.

Right lower lobe:
-Solid subpleural nodule measuring 4 mm on image 180.
-Solid subpleural nodule measuring 2.5 × 1.6 cm with irregular margins and spiculation on images 182-193.

Left lower lobe:
-Peribronchovascular micronodules measuring up to 3 mm on images 136-143.
-Solid subpleural nodule measuring 8 mm on image 142.

In both lungs, ground-glass and solid micronodules measure up to 3 mm. In both lungs, confluent centrilobular emphysema. In the right middle lobe and the left upper and lower lobes, platelike atelectasis. Thick-walled cyst in the right lower lobe measuring up to 2.3 × 1.1 cm on images 180-185. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy.

Heart: Cardiomegaly (cardiothoracic ratio 0.57). Mild pericardial effusion measuring up to 8 mm in thickness. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, severe wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated. Aberrant right subclavian artery.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Diffuse heterogeneous decreased density of vertebral bodies (maximum down to 33 HU).

Included upper abdomen: In the left kidney, a lesion with density 25 HU measuring 2.4 × 1.7 cm (not entirely included in the scan range), a lesion with density 76 HU measuring up to 6 mm, and a lesion with density 21 HU measuring up to 7 mm. In the right kidney, a nonobstructing 2 mm calculus. The left adrenal gland is thickened up to 11 mm. Hypodense lesion (2 HU) in the pancreatic tail measuring up to 1.0 cm. Diverticula of the colonic wall. Cholecystectomy.

Thyroid gland: Lesions measuring up to 8 × 7 mm with density up to 101 HU.
doc_impression:
1. Multiple pulmonary micronodules in both lungs and solid nodules in both lower lobes. Per Fleischner 2017 recommendations, consider CT in 3 months, positron emission tomography and CT, or biopsy of the nodule. Peribronchovascular micronodules in the right middle lobe and left lower lobe may correspond to bronchiolitis.

2. Confluent centrilobular emphysema. Platelike atelectasis in both lungs. Thick-walled cyst in the right lower lobe.

3. Cardiomegaly. Mild pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Left kidney lesions. Per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018, CT or magnetic resonance imaging without and with contrast is recommended (magnetic resonance imaging is preferred).

5. Hemorrhagic or proteinaceous cyst of the left kidney. Per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018, no further evaluation is required. Nonobstructing right renal calculus.

6. Thickening of the left adrenal gland.

7. Cystic lesion of the pancreatic tail. Per Management of Incidental Pancreatic Cysts: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017, follow-up study once every 2 years.

8. Diverticula of the colonic wall.

9. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis.

10. Thyroid lesion. Per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee, 2014, no further follow-up is required.

11. Cholecystectomy.

INDICATION:

COMPARISON: None available / Chest CT on {comparison_date}

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume {dose_ctdi} mGy; total DLP {dose_dlp} mGy-cm

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months:
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right middle lobe:
- Centrilobular micronodules measure up to 3 mm on images 177-194 (tree-in-bud).
Right lower lobe:
-Solid subpleural nodule measuring 2.5 × 1.6 cm with irregular margins and spiculation on images 182-193.
-Solid nodule measuring 4 mm on image 180.
-Solid nodule measuring 3 mm on image 133.
Left lower lobe:
- Centrilobular nodules measuring up to 7 mm on images 136-154 (tree-in-bud).
In both lungs, a few scattered micronodules measure up to 3 mm. Severe confluent centrilobular emphysema. There is subsegmental atelectasis in the right middle lobe. Subsegmental atelectasis in the lingual segment against the background of bronchial secretion. Thick-walled cyst in the right lower lobe measuring up to 2.3 × 1.1 cm on images 180-185. No pleural effusion. Subsegmental and segmental bronchi contain secretion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy. Small hiatal hernia. 

Heart: Cardiomegaly (cardiothoracic ratio 0.57). Mild pericardial effusion measuring up to 8 mm in thickness. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. Fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the ventricles. Calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, severe wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated. Aberrant right subclavian artery.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Diffuse decreased density of vertebral bodies (maximum down to 33 HU). Bone islands of the right scapula, T6, T7, T10, and the right 8th rib.

Included upper abdomen: In the left kidney, a lesion with density 25 HU measures 2.4 × 1.7 cm (not entirely included in the scan range), a lesion with density 76 HU measures up to 6 mm, and a lesion with density 21 HU measures up to 7 mm. In the right kidney, a non nonobstructing 2 mm calculus. 
Right adrenal lesion measures 1.0 cm, 18 HU. Left adrenal lesion measures 1.1 cm, 7 HU. 
Hypodense lesion (2 HU) in the pancreatic tail measures up to 1.0 cm. Diverticula of the colonic wall. Cholecystectomy.

Thyroid gland: Multiple lesions measure up to 8 mm, with density up to 101 HU.
val_impression:
1. Suspicious nodule measures 2.5 × 1.6 cm in the right lower lobe and may be of neoplastic etiology. Consider PET CT or biopsy. 
Multiple solid nodules in both lungs. Centrilobular nodules in the right middle lobe and left lower lobe may correspond to bronchiolitis.

2. Severe confluent centrilobular emphysema. Subsegmental atelectasis in the lingual segment against the background of bronchial secretion. 

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Cardiomegaly. Calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves.

4. Mild pericardial effusion. 

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Diffuse decreased density of vertebral bodies consistent with osteoporosis.

6. Two indeterminate lesions of the left kidney. ACR White Paper recommendations (Herts, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2018;15:264-273) suggest the following: Further evaluation with CT or MR without and with IV contrast recommended. Hemorrhagic or proteinaceous cyst of the left kidney. Per ACR White Paper recommendations, no further evaluation is required. Non Nonobstructing right renal calculus.

7. Right adrenal lesion measures 10 mm, 18 HU. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: For patients without cancer history, follow-up study with adrenal protocol CT in 12 months recommended. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status and exclude pheochromocytoma. For patients with positive cancer history, prompt further evaluation with adrenal protocol CT recommended.
Left adrenal lesion measures 11 mm, 7 HU, benign lesion. No need for further imaging follow-up.

8. Cystic lesion of the pancreatic tail. Per Management of Incidental Pancreatic ACR White Paper recommendations (Megibow, et al. J AM Coll Radiol 2017; 14: 911-923) suggest the following: Follow-up pancreas-protocol CT or MRI with contrast at 2 years and 4 years after the initial discovery of the cyst or after a growing cyst becomes stable again.

9. Thyroid lesion. Per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee, 2014, no further follow-up is required.

INDICATION: Lung nodule.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 12.40 mGy; total DLP 464.36 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Subsegmental atelectasis in the lingular segments of the left lung. In the aerated portions of the lung parenchyma, there are solitary solid nodules and micronodules, in series 5 presented as follows:

In the right middle lobe:
* Nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 145).

In the left upper lobe:
* Nodule measures up to 4 mm (image 120).

Perifissural nodule on the left measures up to 4 mm (image 109).

No pleural effusion. The trachea and central bronchi are patent, with focal wall calcifications.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy. Calcifications in lymph nodes of station 7. The gastric cardia partially prolapses into the posterior mediastinum, forming a small hiatal hernia, with diaphragmatic crural diastasis up to 2.4 cm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial effusion with a layer thickness of about 1.0 cm. Severe coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.0 cm.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Decreased bone density T11-L1 to +90 HU (CT signs of osteoporosis). Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with forming osteophytes.

Included upper abdomen: The kidneys are partially included in the scan range; at the level of the upper group calyces of the left kidney, a calcification measures up to 3 mm. Cholecystectomy.
doc_impression:
1.

Solitary small pulmonary nodules: If risk factors are present, follow-up chest CT may be performed in 12 months; if no such risk factors are present, per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, no further follow-up is required.

2.

Dilation of the main pulmonary artery up to 3.4 cm. This may be a sign of pulmonary hypertension.

3.

Severe coronary calcification. Coronary calcification is identified, qualitatively assessed as mild, moderate, and severe degree. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4.

Small pericardial effusion.

5.

Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with forming osteophytes. CT signs of osteoporosis at the level of the T11-L1 vertebrae.

6.

Small hiatal hernia.

7.

Partially included calcifications at the level of the calyces of the left kidney (differentiate between nephrolithiasis—more likely versus calyceal calcification—less likely).

8.

Cholecystectomy.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months:
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: There are solitary solid nodules and micronodules, in series 5 presented as follows:
In the right middle lobe:
* Nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 145).
In the left upper lobe:
* Nodule measures up to 4 mm (image 120).
Perifissural node on the left measures up to 4 mm (image 109).
In the right middle lobe calcified nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 151).
No consolidation. No pleural effusion. The trachea and central bronchi are patent, with focal wall calcifications, the trachea contains minimal contents. Minimal thickening of the walls of the subsegmental and segmental bronchi of the lower lobes

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy. Calcifications in subcarinal lymph nodes. The gastric cardia partially prolapses into the posterior mediastinum, forming a small hiatal hernia.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial effusion with a layer thickness of about 1.0 cm. Severe coronary calcification. Fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the left ventricle.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.0 cm.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Decreased vertebral body density: T11 - 98 HU, T12 - 90 HU, L1 - 94 HU. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with forming osteophytes.

Included upper abdomen: The kidneys are partially included in the scan range; at the level of the upper group calyces of the left kidney, a calculus measures up to 3 mm. Cholecystectomy.
val_impression:
1. Small pulmonary nodules up to 4 mm in both lungs. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion.

2. Calcified pulmonary nodule and calcifications in subcarinal lymph nodes consistent with the consequences of healed granulomatous disease, no need for follow-up.

3. Minimal thickening of the walls of the subsegmental and segmental bronchi of the lower lobes corresponds to the signs of bronchitis.

4. Dilation of the main pulmonary artery up to 3.4 cm may be a sign of pulmonary hypertension.

5. Coronary calcification is identified, qualitatively assessed as severe degree. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. 
Fat inclusions in the left ventricle may indicate the consequences of coronary heart disease.

6. Small pericardial effusion.


7. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with forming osteophytes. CT signs of osteoporosis at the level of the T11-L1 vertebrae.

8. Small hiatal hernia.

9. Cholecystectomy. The kidneys are partially included in the scan range; at the level of the upper group calyces of the left kidney, a calculus measures up to 3 mm.

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 13.00 mGy; total DLP 486.67 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura:  
The right hemidiaphragm is elevated, the basilar segments of the right lung are compressed, and aeration of the right lower lobe is decreased (visually without significant change). Less pronounced linear atelectasis is present in the right middle lobe and in the basal segments on the left (visually unchanged).  
In the right lower lobe (series 5, image 127), a subpleural focal area of consolidation persists, measuring about 2.5 × 1.3 cm (previously about 2.4 × 1.3 cm), with band-like margins, with small air lucencies up to 4 mm (previously up to 3 mm).  

In the aerated portions of the left lung, solitary solid nodules persist in the same number (series 5):  
In the upper lobe:  
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 59), with calcification, unchanged.  
In the lower lobe:  
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 5 mm (image 161), with calcification, unchanged.  
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 187), with calcification, previously up to 3 mm.  

Solitary calcifications are present in the lungs, the largest in the right upper lobe measuring up to 5 mm.  
The right costal pleura at the level of the posterior segment of the 3rd rib/intercostal space shows focal thickening up to 9 mm (unchanged).  
No pleural effusion.  

Mild atelectasis is noted in the right lower lobe and an infiltrate in a ground-glass pattern. Focal pleural-based parenchymal thickening in the mediobasal part of the right lower lobe measures 1.7 cm. Additionally, hypodense renal nodules are noted in the costophrenic angle. In the lateral part of the mid zone of the left lung, a pleural-based pleural nodule measuring 6 mm is also identified and is stable.  

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.  

Heart: The cardiac chambers are mildly enlarged. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Mild coronary calcification.  

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is dilated in the ascending portion up to 4.6 cm (unchanged), the descending aorta is not dilated, and the walls are calcified. The pulmonary trunk is dilated up to 3.4 cm (unchanged by the author’s measurements).  

Osseous structures and chest wall: Decreased height of the T12 vertebral body up to 32%, decreased density of vertebral bodies T11 and L1 to +84 HU (CT signs of osteoporosis). Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with small osteophytes.  

Included portions of the abdomen are unchanged: Numerous hypodense lesions in the liver with fluid density, the largest at the border of segments 5 and 6 measuring up to 4.1 × 3.3 cm; closer to the paracaval portions of segment 5, a solitary lesion with a peripheral calcification up to 3 mm (series 4, image 211).  
In the lateral limb of the left adrenal gland, a low-density nodular structure (-5 HU) measures about 9 × 9 mm (series 4, image 235).
doc_impression:
1. Subpleural focal consolidation in the right lower lobe may correspond to rounded atelectasis; against this background, the presence or absence of malignant changes cannot be excluded. Continue CT follow-up in 6–12 months. No significant change compared with the prior study. Focal thickening of the right costal pleura at the level of the right upper lobe is unchanged (postinflammatory changes versus; correlate with clinical history).  

2. Solitary solid partially calcified nodules in the left lung are unchanged (likely postinflammatory). If risk factors are present, follow-up chest CT may be performed in 12 months; if no such risk factors are present, per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, no further follow-up is required.  

3. Dilatation of the pulmonary trunk up to 3.4 cm. This may be a sign of pulmonary hypertension.  

4. Cardiomegaly and mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.  

5. Aortic valve calcification.  

6. Aneurysmal dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta (unchanged) and atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.  

7. CT signs of osteoporosis (T11–L1). Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.  

8. Multiple hypodense liver lesions with fluid structure (may correspond to cysts; in a noncontrast study, complete evaluation is not possible), unchanged. Per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee 2017, if no risk factors are present, follow-up is not required.  

9. Small left adrenal nodule with a benign phenotype. Per Management of Incidental Adrenal Masses: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee 2017, follow-up is not required.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months:
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: The right hemidiaphragm is elevated, the basilar segments of the right lung are compressed, several discoid atelectases in the lower lobe, unchanged ground glass area with reticulations supradiaphragmatically. Discoid atelectases are present in the middle lobe and in the left lung. Reticular (fibrous) changes with traction bronchioloectasis in the dorsal parts of both lungs, unchanged.
In the right lower lobe (series 5, image 127), a subpleural area of consolidation measures about 2.2 × 1.1 cm (previously about 1.6 × 1.0 cm), with irregular margins, with gas collections up to 4 mm (previously up to 3 mm).
Solid nodules are presented as follows(series 5):  
In the lower right lobe:  
* Dorsally, against the background of reticulation (fibrosis), multiple nodules (image 83, 97, 102, 122, 129), unchanged.  
* Cavitating nodules measuring up to 6 mm (image 131, 137, 143), unchanged,  may be pseudocavities. 
In the upper left lobe:  
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 59), with calcification, unchanged.  
In the lower left lobe:  
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 5 mm (image 161), with calcification, unchanged.  
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 187), with calcification, unchanged.  
Calcified nodules are present in both lungs, the largest in the right upper lobe measuring up to 5 mm.  
The right costal pleura at the level of the posterior segment of the 3rd rib/intercostal space shows focal thickening up to 9 mm (unchanged).  
No pleural effusion.  

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.  

Heart: The left cardiac chambers are mildly enlarged. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Mild coronary calcification. Mild calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is dilated in the ascending portion up to 4.6 cm (unchanged), the descending aorta is not dilated, and the walls are calcified. The pulmonary trunk is dilated up to 3.4 cm (unchanged).  

Osseous structures and chest wall: Decreased height of the T12 vertebral body up to 32%, decreased density of vertebral bodies T11 and L1 to +84 HU. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with small osteophytes.  

Included portions of the abdomen: Numerous stable hypodense lesions in the liver with fluid density, the largest at the border of segments 5 and 6 measuring up to 4.1 × 3.3 cm; closer to the paracaval portions of segment 5, a solitary lesion with a peripheral calcification up to 3 mm (series 4, image 211).
val_impression:
1. In the right lower lobe, subpleural cavitating pulmonary mass with interval growth in size. Interval growth raises concern for a possible neoplastic etiology, inflammatory changes are less likely. Multiple solid, calcified, cavitating nodules, unchanged. Interstitial changes in the lower lobes, more pronounced on the right.

2. Aneurysmal dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta (unchanged). Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.  

3. Dilatation of the pulmonary trunk up to 3.4 cm. This may be a sign of pulmonary hypertension.  

4. Left heart enlargement. Mild coronary calcification. Mild calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves.

5. Wedge deformity of the T12 vertebral body and decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteoporosis. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.  

6. Multiple hypodense liver lesions with fluid structure (may correspond to cysts), unchanged. Per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee 2017, if no risk factors are present, follow-up is not required.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: Checking aorta and aneurysm size.

COMPARISON: Chest CT on August 15, 2025.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 7.70 mGy; total DLP 279.29 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 2.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:

Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule measuring 3 mm along the minor fissure on image 86 (previously not visualized).

Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule measuring 4 mm on image 120 with cavitation (previously not visualized).
- Calcified granuloma measuring 2 mm on image 128 (no interval change).

Left upper lobe:
- Calcified granuloma measuring 5 mm on image 42 (no interval change).
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 3 mm on image 90 (previously not visualized).

Left lower lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 7 mm on image 96 (previously not visualized).

In the left upper lobe, a peribronchial area of consolidation, series 5, images 82-92 (previously not visualized).
In the left upper lobe, a subpleural area of consolidation, series 5, images 108-123 (no interval change).
Pulmonary markings are heterogeneously increased and distorted, predominantly due to thickening of intralobular and interlobular septa, with more pronounced changes in the peripheral lungs.
Bilateral cysts, variable in size and shape (up to 6 mm), and bronchiectasis.
Bilateral apical caps are present.
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified. Calcifications are present in a few nonenlarged intrathoracic lymph nodes.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Mitral valve replacement.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Wall calcification. The pulmonary trunk is dilated to 3.0 cm.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. The long axis of the spine deviates to the right.

Included abdomen: A calculus in the upper pole of the partially visualized left kidney, measuring up to 1.4 × 0.9 cm, does not cause obstruction.
doc_impression:
1.

Solid nodules and areas of consolidation in both lungs with mixed interval change compared with December 22, 2025 (possibly consistent with chronic coccidioidomycosis).

2.

The predominant pattern of changes in both lungs may correspond to possible usual interstitial pneumonia.

3.

Calcified pulmonary nodules and calcification of mediastinal lymph nodes, consistent with prior infection.

4.

Severe coronary artery calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5.

Mitral valve replacement.

6.

Left renal calculus.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months:
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral diffuse subpleural areas of ground glass opacities, reticulations, and traction bronchiectasis are stable. Bilateral apical scarring is present with traction bronchiectasis.
In the left upper lobe, a peribronchial area of consolidation with ground glass around the periphery, series 5, images 82-92, previously not visualized.
In the left upper lobe, a subpleural area of consolidation, probably with pseudocavitation, is present, series 5, images 108-123 (no interval change).
Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 3 mm on image 86 (previously not visualized).
Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 4 mm on image 120 with cavitation (previously not visualized).
- Calcified granuloma measures 2 mm on image 128 (no interval change).
Left upper lobe:
- Calcified granuloma measures 5 mm on image 42 (no interval change).
- Solid nodules measure 4 mm on images 90-95 (previously not visualized).
Left lower lobe:
- Solid nodules measure up to 6 mm on images 96-99 (previously not visualized).
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified. Calcifications are present in a few nonenlarged intrathoracic lymph nodes.

Heart: Enlargement of the left chambers of the heart is present, with calcifications in the left ventricle. No clinically significant pericardial effusion is present. Coronary calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. Mitral valve prosthesis. Reduced attenuation of the intracardiac blood within the left ventricle, associated with anemia.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. The pulmonary trunk is dilated to 3.0 cm.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine and shoulder joints. Decrease in vertebral body density: T11 - 85 HU, T12 - 80 HU.

Included abdomen: A calculus in the upper pole of the partially visualized left kidney, measures up to 1.4 × 0.9 cm and does not cause obstruction.
val_impression:
1. New area of consolidation in the left upper lobe, new multiple nodules in both lungs, stable area of consolidation, probably with pseudocavitation. Negative interval change compared with December 22, 2025, findings possibly consistent with chronic coccidioidomycosis.

2. Interstitial lung disease may correspond to nonspecific interstitial pneumonia or possible usual interstitial pneumonia.

3. Calcified pulmonary nodules and calcification of mediastinal lymph nodes, consistent with prior infection.

4. Severe coronary artery calcification. Enlargement of the left chambers of the heart, calcifications in the left ventricle. Mitral valve prosthesis. Reduced attenuation of the intracardiac blood within the left ventricle, associated with anemia.

5. Dilated main pulmonary artery (30 mm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

6. Left renal non nonobstructing calculus. 

7. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine and shoulder joints. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteoporosis.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: Pulmonary coccidioidomycosis.

COMPARISON: Chest CT on December 22, 2025.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 7.15 mGy; total DLP 246 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 3.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Hypostatic changes in the posterior basal lung bases. Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and basal segments on the left.  
Mild ground-glass opacities in the right lower lobe (visually unchanged), likely hypoventilatory in nature (the prior study was performed on incomplete inspiration, and the changes were more pronounced).  
Single solid nodules are identified in the lung parenchyma, in series 5 as follows:  
In the right lung:  
In the middle lobe, a nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 103), with calcification, not previously visualized.  
In the left lung:  
Along the interlobar fissure, up to 6 mm (image 113), unchanged.

No pleural effusion. Costal pleura in the right supradiaphragmatic regions with areas of calcification, visually unchanged.  
The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Distal portions of the cardiac pacemaker (implanted in the left pectoral region) are within the lumen of the right heart chambers. Aortic valve calcification. Pericardial fluid measures up to 1.2 cm in thickness. Moderate coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.2 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased density of bone tissue in vertebrae at the T11 level is less than 100 HU (CT signs of osteoporosis). Decreased height and wedge deformity of the T8 vertebral body up to 26%.  
Healing fractures of the posterior segments of the right 5th, 6th, and 7th ribs (at the level of the necks).

Upper abdomen:  
In the liver parenchyma (segment 8), a hypodense lesion with well-defined smooth margins and fluid density measures up to 7 × 6 mm; a similar lesion in segment 2 measures up to 9 × 9 mm (previous visualization was unsatisfactory). The gallbladder is not visualized (removed?), with dilation of the lobar bile ducts up to 1.1 cm, and of the common bile duct up to 1.2 cm; the density of the parenchyma in the subpleural portions of segment 4 is decreased, visually unchanged at the level of the study.

Thyroid gland: Heterogeneous structure due to indistinct hypodense lesions up to 1.2 × 1.2 cm.
doc_impression:
1. Solid 6 mm nodule along the left interlobar pleura, unchanged compared with the prior study. If risk factors are present, follow-up CT of the chest may be performed in 12 months. If no such risk factors, per Society recommendations.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.2 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. Cardiac pacemaker placement.

4. Aortic valve calcification.

5. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

6. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased height of the T8 vertebral body. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis.

7. Hypodense liver lesions, areas of decreased liver parenchymal density, and bile duct dilation: per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee 2017, MRI is recommended in the near term.

8. Thyroid nodules: per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee 2014, thyroid ultrasound is recommended.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Gravitational changes in the posterior basal lung bases. Linear atelectasis in lingular segments, and basal segments on the left, clinically insignificant. Subpleural mild ground-glass opacities in the left upper lobe, unchanged, likely interstitial changes, clinically insignificant.
Solid nodules are identified in the lung parenchyma, in series 5 as follows:  
- In the right middle lobe, a nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 103), not previously visualized.  
- In the left lung, along the interlobar fissure, a nodule measures up to 6 mm (image 113), unchanged, likely perifissural node, no follow-up is required.
No pleural effusion. Costal pleura in the right supradiaphragmatic regions with areas of calcification, unchanged. The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Distal portions of the cardiac pacemaker (implanted in the left pectoral region) are within the lumen of the right heart chambers. Aortic valve calcification. Pericardial fluid measures up to 7 mm in thickness. Moderate coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.2 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased density of bone tissue in vertebrae at the T11 level is less than 100 HU (CT signs of demineralization). Decreased height and wedge deformity of the T8 vertebral body up to 26%.  

Upper abdomen:  
In the liver parenchyma (segment 8), a hypodense lesion with well-defined smooth margins and fluid density measures up to 7 × 6 mm; a similar lesion in segment 2 measures up to 9 × 9 mm (the previous study is not suitable for visualization). The gallbladder is removed, dilatation of the lobar bile ducts up to 1.1 cm, and of the common bile duct up to 1.2 cm, unchanged.

Thyroid gland: Hypodense lesions (6 mm in the left lobe, 4 mm in the right lobe).
val_impression:
1. Solid 6 mm nodule along the left interlobar pleura, likely perifissural node, unchanged compared with the prior study, no follow-up is required. In the right middle lobe, a nodule measures up to 3 mm, not previously visualized. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.2 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension. Consider cardiac echo.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. 

4. Aortic valve calcification. Small pericardial effusion.

5. Cystic liver lesions. ACR White Paper recommendations (Gore, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1429-1437) suggest the following: No need for further follow-up in low risk group.

6. Thyroid nodules up to 6 mm. ACR White Paper recommendations (Hoang, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2015;12:143-150) suggest the following: No need for further follow-up.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
